scenario: notion V6-E retest #2 (path_patterns SQL fix + wildcard /chat*)
summary: V6-A NDJSON block phase OK (user message persists, no AI response). Hard-reload phase: /chat?t=3599aa2d8bdd8018bc2200a96ff2c366&wfv=chat doc requests appear as (failed) status with 0.0 kB body — APF appears to reset the H2 stream but NO warning HTML is delivered. SPA recovers via Service Worker (200, 94 ms) which serves a cached/stub doc. Result: no warning render, no Korean text, no tab title change. SQL+wildcard fix-2 did NOT achieve warning injection. Same outcome as #718/#716.

block_phase.behavior: V6-A NDJSON block confirmed — user bubble persists, no AI streaming response, page transitions /ai?t=new -> /chat?t=<new_id>&wfv=chat as expected

network_observations.post_submit: no doc reload (SPA fetch — request not separately captured because devtools network tab was preserve-log on, but submit-time AI XHR did not surface a warning)

post_reload.name: chat?t=3599aa2d8bdd8018bc2200a96ff2c366&wfv=chat
post_reload.annotation: Document request failed — Response tab: 'Failed to load response data: No resource with given identifier found'. Indicates APF reset/closed the H2 stream rather than substituting warning HTML body.

post_reload.name: chat?t=3599aa2d8bdd8018bc2200a96ff2c366&wfv=chat
post_reload.annotation: Second (retry) doc request — also failed, identical pattern.

post_reload.name: chat?t=3599aa2d8bdd8018bc2200a96ff2c366&wfv=chat
post_reload.annotation: Notion's ServiceWorker intercepted and served a fallback/cached doc, which is what actually rendered. This is NOT the APF warning HTML — it is Notion SW response carrying the chat shell.

network_observations.comparison_to_718: In #718 the doc came back 200 with full 12.0/12.1 kB SSR HTML — APF on_http2_response apparently NEVER intercepted (path_patterns mismatch). In #719 the SQL+wildcard fix changed the behavior: APF NOW DOES touch the stream (visible failure), BUT instead of body-substitution the stream is reset → SW fallback → no warning.

comparison_vs_718.718_outcome: PARTIAL_block_ok_but_reload_doesnt_inject — doc 200, 12.0/12.1 kB SSR HTML served, page_inject_h2 path NOT firing for SSR doc, tab title 'Notion AI | Notion'
comparison_vs_718.719_outcome: PARTIAL_block_ok_but_reload_doesnt_inject — doc (failed) 0 kB then SW fallback, tab title 'Notion AI | Notion'
comparison_vs_718.key_difference: 718: APF DID NOT touch the doc at all (path_patterns mismatch). 719: SQL+wildcard fix-2 made APF target the doc — but instead of delivering warning HTML body it produced stream-reset → SW fallback. Path_patterns now matches; warning HTML emission still broken.
comparison_vs_718.fix2_status: APF reaches the request now but warning HTML substitution does not occur — likely is_page_load classification, response handler bailout, or warning body being dropped before flush. Recommend: (a) inspect engine log for [APF:H2_RESP] entries during 09:25-09:28 KST window for path /chat?t=3599...&wfv=chat (b) check if on_http2_response does early-return after path match guard (c) verify warning HTML buffer is being written to upstream stream not consumed by error path.

console_errors_post_reload: opfs-sahpool: NoModificationAllowedError: Failed to execute 'createSyncAccessHandle' on 'FileSystemFileHandle' (multiple)
Uncaught (in promise) Error: Failed to load a required record: user_root, 356d872b-594c-8152-a77d-00025150fd17, undefined (loadRecordModel chain)

recommendation_to_dev: Fix-2 wildcard /chat* now causes APF to engage with the SPA doc URL but the engine resets the H2 stream rather than returning warning HTML body. Mine engine logs for [APF:H2_RESP] in 09:25-09:28 KST window for path /chat?t=3599aa2d8bdd8018bc2200a96ff2c366&wfv=chat. Investigate: (a) is_page_load value, (b) early-return condition after path match, (c) whether the warning body is queued but the upstream H2 frame writer is short-circuiting before flush, (d) interaction with last_pii_block_at_ms throttle. The 200 entry on the third Doc row is Notion SW fallback (ServiceWorker initiator), not the APF warning.
